mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) pushes up inflation expectations, which mechanically raises the term premium and nominal yields on 10-year Treasuries over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of monthly CPIAUCSL changes vs. subsequent 20-day yield changes is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year period, or a Granger causality test fails at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals labor market weakness and impending recession, triggering safe-haven demand for long-term Treasuries and compressing yields over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, 60-day forward yield changes are positive in more than 40% of historical cases, or the average yield change is not negative at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of elevated fed funds (FEDFUNDS > 3.5) and declining housing starts (HOUST < 1300) indicates that monetary tightening is dampening interest-rate-sensitive demand, reducing growth expectations and lowering 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both threshold conditions are met simultaneously, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative in fewer than 60% of instances over a 5-year rolling window, or the average change is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion as signaled by rising GDPC1, strong retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce expectations of overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and raising 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive output gap
failure_condition: In expansion regimes defined by GDPC1 above its rolling 5-year trend, the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 20-day yield changes is not positive and statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals a contraction in manufacturing and aggregate supply, raising recession fears and driving investors into long-term Treasuries, which pushes 10-year yields down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After INDPRO falls below 100, 20-day yield changes are positive in more than 30% of instances, or the mean yield change is not negative at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, which fuels wage inflation and overall demand, increasing the term premium and lifting 10-year yields over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The long-term rolling correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and 60-day yield changes is not positive over any 10-year window, or the signal fails to predict yield direction with accuracy above 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is above 320 and unemployment (UNRATE) is below 4.0%, the economy exhibits classic late-cycle overheating, which forces the Fed to raise rates aggressively, pushing 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both threshold conditions are met, the subsequent 20-day yield change is positive in fewer than 60% of instances over a 5-year rolling period, or the average change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1200 thousand units signal a sharp contraction in residential investment, typically preceding a broader downturn that boosts safe-asset demand and lowers 10-year yields over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: After HOUST drops below 1200, the 5-day forward yield change is negative in fewer than 60% of instances over the historical sample, or the average change is not statistically negative at the 10% level.
